signals.title: USD/CAD — Geopolitical Shock + US CPI
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3719 as Strait of Hormuz closure pushes WTI crude up +4.10% to $102.1, while CAD dives on risk-off sentiment ahead of US CPI. Despite the oil rally typically supporting CAD, the 150bp Fed-BoC carry advantage and safe-haven USD demand dominate. CAD COT LF net short -46,889 (26.4% long) reflects heavy institutional bearishness. Implication: CAD remains under pressure despite energy tailwinds as market prioritizes US inflation risk and geopolitical hedging.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 113.893 triggered by a 0.855% G8 spread between the week's leader (AUD) and laggard (JPY). The 360bp RBA-BoJ carry premium sustains the bid despite JPY intervention warnings near 157.75. AUD COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) shows crowded institutional positioning, while retail is 84% short. Implication: Institutional carry-chasing vs retail fading creates a divergence; the trend remains intact until VIX exceeds 25.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (extreme 18.5% long) shows specs heavily fading the currency despite a hawkish RBNZ market bias. With the RBNZ meeting on 27 May signaling a hike bias, a crowded spec short unwind risk is elevated if the central bank delivers. NZD/USD at 0.5944 remains supported by the 2s10s bear steepener regime (+55.5bp) which favors higher-beta carry. Implication: Short-squeeze risk on hawkish RBNZ confirmation.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Correlation Break + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation at +0.597 vs norm of +0.150 (z=-2.19σ) indicates the pair is over-sensitized to US Treasury moves. USD/JPY at 157.672 is testing levels where BoJ intervention risk rises, as noted by BBH. JPY COT LF net short -61,340 confirms a crowded carry trade. Implication: JPY safe-haven function is currently suppressed by the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry gap; watch for verbal intervention to trigger a sharp squeeze toward 155.00.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Retail Sentiment Divergence
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7818 with retail 83% long while COT LF specs remain net short -7,392 (30.5% long). This extreme retail long positioning against institutional shorts suggests a contrarian downside risk if US CPI underwhelms or geopolitical tensions trigger a CHF safe-haven bid. The 375bp Fed-SNB carry advantage currently keeps the pair bid, but retail capitulation often precedes a reversal. Implication: Upside exhaustion risk if 0.7900 is not cleared post-CPI.
